title: warp-types: Type-Safe GPU Warp Programming via Linear Typestate
description: A type system that prevents shuffle-from-inactive-lane bugs in GPU warp programming by tracking active lane masks at compile time. Diverged warps literally cannot call shuffle — the method does not exist on the type. Zero runtime overhead verified at Rust MIR, LLVM IR, and NVIDIA PTX levels. Real GPU execution on NVIDIA H200 SXM and RTX 4000 Ada (4 typed kernels PASS on both); AMD MI300X mask-correctness verified via HIP. v0.3.1 fixes WarpBuilder target-cpu (PTX 6.0 support), adds ballot setp/selp workaround, and hardens warp_kernel macro.
notes: Includes: Rust implementation (317 unit + 50 example + 28 doc = 395 tests), SimWarp multi-lane simulator, Lean 4 formalization (31 theorems, zero sorry, zero axioms — progress, preservation, all four §5.1 loop rules, nested merge, letPair), cargo-integrated GPU compilation pipeline (#[warp_kernel] + WarpBuilder), C++20 interop header (include/warp_types.h), CUB-equivalent typed warp primitives, and 8 worked bug examples covering 21 documented bugs across 16 real-world projects.
